titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Floyd E. Risvold Collection of James Edgar Cady Correspondence
(Risvold) Floyd E., Collection of James Edgar Cady Correspondence
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2020 at 04:00

did.unittitle: Floyd E. Risvold Collection of James Edgar Cady Correspondence

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Letters by James Edgar Cady to family and friends in Dodge City, Steele County, Minnesota, August 1862 to July 1865, during his military service with 8th Minnesota Infantry Regiment, Company G, as well as a tintype portrait of Cady in August 1862. Letters describe his service chiefly based at Fort Abercrombie, Dakota Territory, during the Dakota War in 1862 through an expedition led by Alfred Sully against the Dakota in 1864 as well as Cady's movement through Tennessee and North Carolina during the United States Civil War in 1864-1865. Most of the letters are addressed generally to his family and friends with a significant quantity explicitly directed to his parents and siblings. Floyd E. Risvold collected the letters and tintype as well as related material he used to exhibit the letters and accompanying illustrated envelopes at philatelic shows around 1960. The collection includes copies of an article by Risvold based on the letters: Floyd E. Risvold, "Indians and Rebels," Stamps (September 7, 1963).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from William Reese Co. (Spink Shreves Galleries' sale, 2010 January 27-29, lot 311) on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2010.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Letters by James Edgar Cady, 1862-1865. II. Visual Resources, 1862-1960. III. Publication and Exhibition Material, 1960-1963.

bioghist.head: James Edgar Cady (1844-1916)
bioghist.p: James Edgar Cady was born in Herkimer County, New York, in 1844, and was the eldest son of Edgar Cady (1817-1901) and Emily Augusta Hines Cady (1819-1897). In August 1862, he enlisted at Lake City, Minnesota, for a three-year term with the 8th Minnesota Infantry Regiment, Company G. Cady served as a private during the Dakota War in 1862 through an expedition led by Alfred Sully against the Dakota in 1864 as well as serving in the United States Civil War with movement through Tennessee and North Carolina in 1864 until he mustered out in July 1865.

After his military service, Cady was a farmer and apiarist at Merton and Medford in Steele County, Minnesota. In 1893, Cady married May Emily Smith Cady Schacht (1870-1955) and they had six children: Alfred Wilber Cady (1896-1896), Merle Wilton Cady (1898-1971), Cecil Rhodes Cary (1900-1970), Sir Irl Cady (1901-1966), Irma Violette Cady Barnes Kenppainen Finckh (1907-1992), Robert William Cady (1909-1956); they divorced around 1910.

bioghist.head: Floyd E. Risvold (1912-2009)
bioghist.p: Floyd Eugene Risvold was a manuscript collector, dealer, philatelist, and historian from Minnesota with an interest in Western Americana. He was born in Milltown, Wisconsin, the eldest son of Oswald Eugene Risvold (1886-1957) and Marie Henrietta Lund Risvold (1884-1969) and raised in the Twin Cities, Minnesota.

Risvold served with the United States Coast and Geodetic Survey, 1932-1946. He then joined O. E. Risvold & Sons, the infant products distribution company founded in 1930 by his father, which operated until his family sold the company in 1984 and he retired as the managing partner for the firm.

Publications by Risvold include his The Minnesota Territory in Postmarks, Letters and Covers ( Chicago: Collectors' Club of Chicago, 1985) as well as editor of Louis J. Weichmann, A True History of the Assassination of Abraham Lincoln and the Conspiracy of 1865 ( New York: Alfred A. Knopf, 1975), which received the Benjamin Barondess Award from the Civil War Round Table of New York in 1976.

In 1941, Risvold married Sophie Evelyn Lindstrom Risvold (1915-2007) and they had four children: Diane Marie Risvold Pearson (born approximately 1944), Kathleen Sandra Risvold Cathcart (born 1947), Douglas Floyd Risvold (1952-1953) and Howard Eugene Risvold (1952-1953).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Floyd E. Risvold Collection of James Edgar Cady Correspondence. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters by James Edgar Cady to family and friends in Dodge City, Steele County, Minnesota, August 1862 to July 1865, during his military service with 8th Minnesota Infantry Regiment, Company G, as well as a tintype portrait of Cady in August 1862. Letters describe his service chiefly based at Fort Abercrombie, Dakota Territory, during the Dakota War in 1862 through a punitive expedition led by Alfred Sully in 1864 as well as Cady's movement through Tennessee and North Carolina during the United States Civil War in 1864-1865. Most of the letters are addressed generally to his family and friends with a significant quantity explicitly directed to his parents and siblings. Floyd E. Risvold collected the letters and tintype as well as related material he used to exhibit the letters and accompanying illustrated envelopes at philatelic shows around 1960. The collection includes copies of an article by Risvold based on the letters: Floyd E. Risvold, "Indians and Rebels," Stamps (September 7, 1963).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Floyd E. Risvold Collection of James Edgar Cady Correspondence is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: United States. Army. Minnesota Infantry Regiment, 8th (1862-1865) Company G

did.unittitle: Correspondence by James Edgar Cady

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters by James Edgar Cady to family and friends in Dodge City, Steele County, Minnesota, August 1862 to July 1865, although the location of the recipient is not always extant. In addition to generally addressed correspondence by Cady, this series includes letters explicitly addressed to his parents, Emily Augusta Hines Cady (1819-1897) and Edgar Cady (1817-1901), as well as to his siblings, Charles William Cady (1846-1828), George B. Cady (1848-1870), Ebenezer Cady (1850-1870), Harriet Maria Cady (1851-1936), and Hobart Cady (1856-1940).

Many letters are accompanied by typescripts. Many original letters or portions of letters were not extant on acquisition by the beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library and only exist as typescripts, which includes the bulk of letters written at Fort Abercrombie, Dakota Territory in 1863.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter written on versos of discrete valentines that depict "a raw recruit" and "to a dragoon" and each includes verse.

scopecontent.p: Includes manuscript poem, "One Year Ago To-Night," by James Edgar Cady, which does not appear in the typed transcript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cady misidentified the site as "Sweet Swamp Cross Roads"; it is in Lenoir County, North Carolina.

Visual Resources

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into two subseries: Photographs and Picture Postcards.

did.unittitle: Photographs
Tintype in a case, of James Edgar Cady

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamps on mat, "Constitution and Union" and imagery including United States flags and drums as well as "Scovill M'f'g. Co. Superior"
Accompanied by a black-and-white copy photograph of the tintype

did.unittitle: Copy Photographs of Prints at the Minnesota Historical Society
Unidentified photographer, Camp of 6th Iowa Cavalry & Wagon Corrals near Fort Berthold (Sully's Expedition of 1864)
Unidentified artist, Battle of Tah-Kah-O-Ku-Ty, The Place Where They Kill Deer, Fought July 28th, 1864
Picture Postcards
Fort Abercrombie, North Dakota, 1863 (Chicago, Illinois: H. G. Zimmerman & Company)
Fort Ridgely, 18 Miles Northwest of New Ulm, Minn., Besieged During Indian Massacre in 1862 (New Ulm, Minnesota: Philip Liesch)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color reproduction of an 1892 painting by Anton Gág (1858-1908)

did.unittitle: Post, Sauk Centre, Minn., 1863, Occupied by Co. B, 8th Minn. (St. Paul, Minnesota: Pioneer Press Company for Fiesler & McNeice, Sauk Centre, Minnesota)
Publication and Exhibition Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes material related to the publication, Floyd E. Risvold, "Indians and Rebels," Stamps (September 7, 1963), and material related to the exhibition of he letters and accompanying illustrated envelopes at philatelic shows around 1960.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Publication and Exhibition Material.

did.unittitle: Publication
Risvold, Floyd E., "Indians and Rebels," Stamps (September 7, 1963): 346-348, 350

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes tear sheets of the article as well as a photocopy of it on cardstock

did.unittitle: Exhibition Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes material used by Risvold to exhibit portions of the James Edgar Cady collection including discrete correspondence, envelopes, and visual resources at philatelic conventions. Photocopies of the archival material housed elsewhere in the collection accompany the exhibition mounts. This guide also references the location of extant archival material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by the material displayed on the mount.

did.unittitle: Patriotic covers, 1861-1865

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mount for illustrated envelopes; envelope dated April 28, 1862, not extant
